key_insights.insight: Mental resilience is crucial; separate mental health from mental illness and stop over-labeling normal life challenges as mental illness.
key_insights.quote: I think we should separate mental health from mental illness I don't think mental health is an issue to even be debated particularly we all all have mental health, but if you have a mental illness, you need help, you need treatment.

key_insights.insight: It's important to have your own opinion on world events, even if that opinion is not popular, and to seek information from reliable sources.
key_insights.quote: Opinions to me are the spice of life if you don't have an opinion there's something wrong with you to me you've got to care about what's happening in the world and you've got to work out what you think about it.

key_insights.insight: You will learn more from failures than successes.
key_insights.quote: I've learned more from failures than success.Success is easy.

key_insights.insight: When you lose a big job, take your time to clear your head and recalibrate before jumping into something else.
key_insights.quote: I always advise people,when they lose a big job,take your time. Just go and clear your head. You'll get offered loads of things, but don't react to it. Let the dust settle.

key_insights.insight: Confidence and self-belief are vital for achieving goals; back yourself no matter who you are facing.
key_insights.quote: Confidence in yourself, self-belief. I think the one thing I have is a lot of self-belief i'm firmly i remember a friend of mine kevin peterson the cricketer his big mantra with himself when he played cricket for england was back yourself back yourself whoever you're facing.

key_insights.insight: The woke movement is a form of fascism because it prevents free thinking and disagreement.
key_insights.quote: The modern-day woke is a form of fascism.You will abide by our rules or you get destroyed.

key_insights.insight: Don't let normal life stuff drag you down and that it is important to maintain a positive outlook.
key_insights.quote: Take one thing away. Get running. Get moving. Be positive. Don't let normal life stuff drag you down.

actionable_advice.advice: When faced with bullying, stand up for yourself rather than showing fear or weakness.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take decisive action by mirroring the bully's intensity and refusing to back down, setting boundaries and not being intimidated.

actionable_advice.advice: When you lose a job, or experience hardship, take time to reset and recalibrate.
actionable_advice.how_to: Don't react immediately to new opportunities. Clear your head, reflect on what you truly want, and consider the situation before jumping back in.

Face life's challenges head-on with persistence and determination, drawing strength from past successes and understanding setbacks are temporary. When faced with a setback, get knocked down, and get back up.

memorable_stories: Piers describes being bullied at comprehensive school for his double-barreled surname and how his brother Jeremy stood up for him.
Piers recounts his experience with Long COVID, highlighting how debilitating the lack of energy and brain fog was and emphasizing the importance of health.

Piers tells the story of how he argued with a vegan about a vegan sausage roll, leading to the company selling a billion dollars worth more of their products that year, and later being thanked by the CEO.
contrarian_claims: Society is spending too much time encouraging a kind of wallowing in self-pity and weakness, which is demonstrably not working.
The public wants someone to cancel cancel culture.
Most people claiming to be triggered are not really triggered but are faking, and we should call that out.
There is an agenda to destroy historical figures such as Winston Churchill because they don't adhere to modern day values.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Piers Morgan: Dealing With Repeat Failure, Death Threats & Regrets
